summary: postcss-minify-selector-parser typosquats postcss-selector-parser and ships an AES-encrypted JS payload that is decrypted and executed via new Function
details: The package name impersonates the legitimate postcss-selector-parser. src/selector-parser.js simply re-exports the real package (`module.exports = require('postcss-selector-parser')`), providing a working CSS parser as cover while the package's main export adds a `run` function that decrypts and executes an opaque embedded blob. src/config/defaults.js ships DEFAULT_FINAL_ENCODED_TEXT (a ~10KB AES-GCM ciphertext) together with the hardcoded passphrase 'default-dev-passphrase' and salt 'encode-npm-c-salt'. src/pipeline/custom-codec-pipeline.js:55 decrypts that blob and runs the plaintext as JavaScript with full Node privileges via `new Function('require', runnable)(require)`. The package exports `run` (alias of runDefaultDecodedFunction) and the generic helper `decodeAndRunPlain`, making the public API a turnkey code-execution surface. runtime/lib.min.js is a one-liner `require('../src').run();`, and package.json wires it to both `npm start` and `npm run decode`; scripts/cjs-runner.js (and the root cjs-runner.js) provide a second trigger that calls `runDefaultDecodedFunction()` directly. Any consumer who calls run(), runs npm start, runs npm run decode, or invokes the cjs-runner files executes attacker-chosen code under the installer's Node process. Encrypting the payload with a key shipped in the same package has no legitimate purpose other than evading static scanners.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
